notes: Run this benchmark from the repository root.
Scenarios avoid real GitHub writes and require a concrete artifact in the copied benchmark workspace.

scenarios.title: Inline review routing
scenarios.purpose: Check that the babysit-pr skill routes GitHub inline review feedback through the thread-aware GitHub comment handler.
scenarios.userInput: Use the local Codex skill "babysit-pr" if it helps. In this copied workspace, inspect plugins/babysit-pr/skills/babysit-pr/SKILL.md and write .plugin-eval-benchmark/babysit-pr.md with the exact steps you would follow when CodeRabbit leaves an inline review thread on a GitHub PR. The artifact must explicitly state that GitHub inline review feedback routes through github:gh-address-comments and that a top-level PR comment is not an acceptable reply to an inline review thread.
successChecklist: The artifact names github:gh-address-comments for GitHub inline review feedback.
The artifact rejects top-level PR comments for inline review thread replies.

scenarios.title: Continuous monitoring boundary
scenarios.purpose: Check that babysit-pr distinguishes ordinary waiting from a true human blocker.
scenarios.userInput: Use the local Codex skill "babysit-pr" if it helps. In this copied workspace, inspect plugins/babysit-pr/skills/babysit-pr/SKILL.md and write .plugin-eval-benchmark/babysit-pr.md with a PR babysitting loop for a PR that is green but waiting in merge queue. The artifact must say to keep polling until MERGED or a concrete unfixable human gate is proven, not to stop merely because checks, bot review, or merge queue are pending.
successChecklist: The artifact says to continue monitoring through pending checks, bot review, or merge queue states.
The artifact defines a real blocker as a concrete unfixable human gate.
